2604.13248.title: GeoVision-Enabled Digital Twin for Hybrid Autonomous-Teleoperated Medical Responses
2604.13248.paper_summary: Remote medical response systems are increasingly being deployed to support emergency care in disaster-affected and infrastructure-limited environments. Enabled by GeoVision capabilities, this paper presents a Digital Twin architecture for hybrid autonomous-teleoperated medical response systems. The proposed framework integrates perception and adaptive navigation with a Digital Twin, synchronized in real-time, that mirrors system states, environmental dynamics, patient conditions, and mission objectives. Unlike traditional ground control interfaces, the Digital Twin provides remote clinical and operational users with an intuitive, continuously updated virtual representation of the platform and its operational context, enabling enhanced situational awareness and informed decision-making.
2604.13248.paper_summary_zh: 遠程醫療應對系統越來越多地被部署，以支持在災害影響和基礎設施有限的環境中的緊急護理。<lb/>本論文利用GeoVision的能力，提出了一種混合自主-遙控醫療應對系統的數位雙胞胎架構。<lb/>所提出的框架將感知和自適應導航與數位雙胞胎整合，實時同步，反映系統狀態、環境動態、病人狀況和任務目標。<lb/>與傳統的地面控制介面不同，數位雙胞胎為遠程臨床和操作用戶提供了一個直觀的、持續更新的虛擬表示，展示了平台及其操作背景，從而增強了情境意識和知情決策能力。
2604.13248.author: Parham Kebria et.al.
2604.13248.authors: Parham Kebria, Soheil Sabri, Laura J Brattain
